title: Catalog/reference site (browse/search/download + comprehension evidence)
context: For downstream consumers (AAC platform maintainers, facility signage teams, humanitarian orgs) to discover, evaluate, and download symbols, open-pictograms needs a static catalog/reference site. The site must expose per-symbol comprehension evidence — not just show the icons — so that users can see the testing data that backs each symbol. The site is a static reference, not a SaaS: it generates from the build pipeline and is deployed to a static host.
objective: Build and publish a static catalog/reference site that allows users to browse, search, filter, and download symbols, with per-symbol pages showing the comprehension score, cultural-review status, and metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Site is generated by the CI build pipeline (code-002) from the metadata records and exported assets; regenerates automatically on every merged PR.
Site supports: browse by category, keyword search across concept IDs and labels, filter by safety class and comprehension status; download of individual SVG/PNG and the full OBF set.

Each symbol has a per-symbol page displaying: the SVG, category, multilingual labels, comprehension score (method, n, population, correct%, date), cultural-review status, safety class (with disclaimer if safety-aligned), and attribution/license.
Site is accessible (WCAG AA): all images have alt text, page structure uses semantic HTML, and the design is operable by keyboard and screen reader.
Site is a static build (no server-side logic or personal data collected); licensed MIT; deployed and publicly accessible.
resources: Creative Commons CC-BY-4.0 (symbol assets); MIT License (site code)
output: pipeline/catalog-site/ (static site generator integrated into the CI build); deployed public catalog URL documented in README; site code licensed MIT, symbol assets CC-BY-4.0. PR opened against main.
